name: Parallax Scroll 2
summary: A three-column image grid with parallax scroll effects that transform and rotate images as users scroll.
detailedDescription: Parallax Scroll 2 creates a responsive image gallery with three columns that apply different parallax transformations based on scroll position. The component divides images into three equal parts, displaying each part in a vertical column. The first and third columns apply scroll-based transformations using Framer Motion's useScroll and useTransform hooks - the first column translates upward and leftward while rotating counter-clockwise, while the third column translates upward and rightward while rotating clockwise. The middle column remains static, creating visual contrast. All transformations are tied to scroll progress within a scrollable container with a 40rem height. Images are displayed as 320px tall cards with rounded corners and object-fit styling. The layout uses a max-width 5xl container with generous padding (py-40, px-10) and 10-unit gaps between columns. This creates a dynamic gallery experience where images appear to move and rotate at different rates as users scroll, adding depth and visual interest to image showcases or portfolios.
purpose: Display image galleries with dynamic scroll-based animations
Create visual depth through differential parallax movement
Showcase portfolios or product images with engaging motion
behavior: Divides images into three columns with equal distribution
Applies scroll-based Y translation to first and third columns
Rotates first column counter-clockwise and third column clockwise during scroll
Translates first column leftward and third column rightward
Keeps middle column static for visual contrast
Uses scroll container offset for parallax calculations
visualCharacteristics: Outer columns rotate and translate on scroll
Generous vertical padding for scroll space
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
